items.headline: Mag 7 super-week Apr 29 after-close actual results
items.diff_narrative: **New: Mag 7 super-week Apr 29 after-close actual results — MSFT $82.9B revenue +18%, Azure +40%, AI run-rate $37B (+123%), capex guide >$40B; GOOGL $109.9B +22%, Cloud $20B +63%, +6% AH; META -7% AH on capex shortfall + user-growth disappointment; AMZN mixed beat-miss** — Yesterday's report previewed (consensus EPS / rev guides). Today's report has actuals. Microsoft FY26 Q3 *beat* the $80.65-$81.75B guide (came in at $82.9B), Azure landed at the high end of the +37-38% cc guide (+40% reported), and the AI run-rate disclosure of $37B is the first time a hyperscaler has put a per-AI-business run rate number in the headline release as a single line item. Alphabet's $37.7B unrealized-equity-gain spike inflated reported net income +81% but was not core ops, an important caveat for the AI-revenue-disclosure thesis. Meta's -7% AH was the cleanest single-print Mag 7 reset in the cycle.

items.headline: FOMC Apr 29 actual
items.diff_narrative: **New: FOMC Apr 29 actual — rates held 3.50-3.75%, 8-4 dissent (highest since Oct-1992); Powell delivered final press conference but is staying on Board of Governors until Trump-admin Fed investigation completes "with transparency and finality"; Warsh nomination advanced from Senate Banking; Mideast war added to statement** — Yesterday's report previewed the meeting (consensus 100% hold). Today's report has actuals. The 8-4 vote is the structural shock: not since Greenspan's October 1992 FOMC has there been four dissenters in a single meeting; Miran wanted -25bp, Hammack/Kashkari/Logan opposed easing-bias inclusion. Powell-staying-on-Board is the larger market-structure surprise — it converts the May-15 Chair-handoff narrative from "Powell exits" to "Powell remains on Board through 2028 as a check on Fed investigation". Apr 29 close: S&P 7,135.95 (-0.04%), Nasdaq 24,673.24 (+0.04%), Dow 48,861.81 (-0.57%) — flat-tape compared to Apr 28's selloff.

items.headline: AAPL Q2 FY26 prints Apr 30 after-close
items.diff_narrative: **New: AAPL Q2 FY26 prints Apr 30 after-close — consensus EPS $1.95 / rev $109.7B (iPhone ~$56.5B, Services ~$30B); JP Morgan high-side $112.7B / iPhone $59.5B; Tim Cook → Executive Chair Sept 1, John Ternus succeeds** — Yesterday's report flagged the date but did not deepen the consensus or the CEO-handoff frame. Today's report adds both. The Cook-Ternus handoff is now the second 2026 leadership transition in the Mag 7 cohort (Powell at Fed Chair was the first); markets read both transitions through the same continuity-vs-discontinuity lens.

items.headline: Apr 28-29 CVE wave deepens
items.diff_narrative: **New: Apr 28-29 CVE wave deepens — CVE-2026-40967 Spring AI FilterExpressionConverter injection (CVSS 8.6, Apr 28) is the first 2026 CVE squarely in the LLM-tooling supply chain at the RAG query plane; CVE-2026-3854 GitHub Enterprise Server RCE (CVSS 8.7, Apr 28) — one git push executes arbitrary commands; CVE-2026-34965 Cockpit CMS authenticated RCE (CVSS 8.8, Apr 29)** — Yesterday's report covered the Apr 28 Milesight + Totolink + CISA KEV Apr 28 wave. Today's report adds three more: Spring AI's keys/values not properly escaped when translating filter expressions to vector-store query languages (this is structurally the same shape as the SGLang Jinja2/SSTI primitive — it's a model-tooling-layer injection class now), GitHub's git infrastructure RCE (single push to any repo, including a self-created one), and Cockpit CMS in /cockpit/collections/save_collection.

items.headline: AWS Bedrock AgentCore Runtime adds Node.js as managed language runtime Apr 28
items.diff_narrative: **New: AWS Bedrock AgentCore Runtime adds Node.js as managed language runtime Apr 28 — second supported language after Python** — Yesterday's report covered AgentCore Managed Harness 4-region preview + CLI 14-region GA. Today's report adds Node.js runtime support as the next step. Pairs with the same-day Novita Sandbox + Multica v0.2.19/v0.2.20 in the agent-runtime layer hardening into Apr 30.

items.headline: Multica v0.2.19 (Apr 28) + v0.2.20 (Apr 29 09:40 UTC)
items.diff_narrative: **New: Multica v0.2.19 (Apr 28) + v0.2.20 (Apr 29 09:40 UTC) — superseding v0.2.18 (Apr 27)** — Yesterday's report had v0.2.18. Today's report adds two more minors in 48 hours; pairs with the Novita Sandbox launch and the AgentCore Node.js runtime in the same window. Multica is news-driven coverage (per project memory) — these two minors qualify on cadence alone, and the orchestrator-skills surface continues to deepen alongside ATT&CK v19's agentic-AI naming wave.

items.headline: MITRE ATT&CK v19 lands Apr 28
items.diff_narrative: **New: MITRE ATT&CK v19 lands Apr 28 — splits Defense Evasion (TA0005) into Stealth + Impair Defenses; v5.4 framework adds AI-agent context poisoning / tool invocation abuse / MCP server compromise / malicious agent deployment as named techniques** — Yesterday's report did not cover ATT&CK v19. The Defense Evasion split is structural; the agentic-AI technique additions are the validation of the prior-day Future-section thesis ("agent-skills attack surface lands its own MITRE-ATT&CK-style sub-matrix"). The first prediction is now partially landed — v19 names the techniques but does not yet ship a skill-marketplace-poisoning sub-matrix; that's the next step.

items.headline: SuiteCloud Agent Skills announced Apr 28 at SuiteConnect SF
items.diff_narrative: **New: SuiteCloud Agent Skills announced Apr 28 at SuiteConnect SF — Oracle NetSuite ships skills as a product with signed-distribution security frame** — Yesterday's report covered AI Dev 26 SF (Apr 28-29); today's report adds the SuiteConnect SF announcement on the same day. Oracle NetSuite is the first major non-foundation-lab vendor publishing skills-as-a-product, and the security frame uses signed-distribution as the default — a direct inference-target for the Future-1 prediction.

items.headline: Apr 28-29 indirect-prompt-injection traction
items.diff_narrative: **New: Apr 28-29 indirect-prompt-injection traction — Google Online Security Blog reports +32% relative malicious-category increase Nov-2025 → Feb-2026 in observed IPI payloads; 10 fresh IPI payloads documented (financial fraud / data destruction / API-key theft); Forcepoint corroborates with independent payload set; arxiv 2604.23374 NeuroTaint (Apr 24) is the first taint-tracking framework purpose-built for LLM agents** — Yesterday's report mentioned Apr 24-27 IPI carry-forward at a high level. Today's report quantifies (+32% Nov-2025→Feb-2026) and structurally connects the Google + Forcepoint + NeuroTaint countermove arc.

items.headline: GPT-5.5 / GPT-5.5 Pro carry-forward into Apr 28-30
items.diff_narrative: **New: GPT-5.5 / GPT-5.5 Pro carry-forward into Apr 28-30 — released Apr 23, API access Apr 24 ($5/M input, $30/M output, 1M context); ChatGPT Plus/Pro/Business/Enterprise + Codex; CarPlay voice access; Fast answers; OpenAI projects 122M paid subscribers by 2026 with new $8/month plan; ad pilot at $100M annualized within weeks (projection: $2.4-$2.5B in 2026)** — Yesterday's report did not cover GPT-5.5 detail. The $8 plan + ad-revenue projection layer onto the Apr 28 WSJ OpenAI-revenue-miss thesis: OpenAI's response to the WSJ scoop is to monetize the long-tail user base.

items.headline: Anthropic 1M context beta retires Apr 30
items.diff_narrative: **New: Anthropic 1M context beta retires Apr 30 — Sonnet 4.5 / Sonnet 4 users must migrate to Sonnet 4.6 / Opus 4.6 for 1M context; Message Batches API max_tokens cap raised to 300k for Opus 4.6 / Sonnet 4.6** — Yesterday's report did not surface this date specifically. The retirement is today; users without migration will fail at deployment.

items.headline: ChatGPT outage Apr 29
items.diff_narrative: **New: ChatGPT outage Apr 29 — hundreds of users reported downtime during Mag 7 print window; status page recorded incident** — Single-day operational issue but coincided with the Mag 7 super-week tape; markets read this as a side story to the Apr 28 WSJ OpenAI-revenue-miss thesis (operational-reliability tail).

items.headline: KV-cache compression utility wave (TurboQuant + SGLang #21419/#23134 + PolyKV + NVIDIA kvpress + KVTC + TriAttention) carry into Apr 28-30
items.diff_narrative: **Continuing: KV-cache compression utility wave (TurboQuant + SGLang #21419/#23134 + PolyKV + NVIDIA kvpress + KVTC + TriAttention) carry into Apr 28-30** — Carry-forward; Foundry Local (GA Apr 9, ~20MB native library, WinML execution providers) is the missing Windows-default integration point; the obvious Q2-2026 step for Microsoft is to surface a TurboQuant flag in the Foundry Catalog.

items.headline: ClawHavoc (1,184 malicious ClawHub skills) + Cisco RSAC 36%-prompt-injection-rate
items.diff_narrative: **Continuing: ClawHavoc (1,184 malicious ClawHub skills) + Cisco RSAC 36%-prompt-injection-rate** — Carry-forward; Apr 28 Novita Sandbox + ATT&CK v19 + SuiteCloud Agent Skills signed-distribution form the structural countermove. The threat-class is now first-class infrastructure.

items.headline: SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) still unpatched 11+ days post-disclosure into Apr 30
items.diff_narrative: **Continuing: SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) still unpatched 11+ days post-disclosure into Apr 30** — Maintainer-coordination yields no fix; v0.5.10 ships exploitable. Cross-server class to vLLM CVE-2025-61620 + LMDeploy CVE-2026-33626 + Apr 28's CVE-2026-40967 Spring AI FilterExpressionConverter (same model-tooling-layer-injection shape).

items.headline: Cerebras IPO mid-May 2026 at $22B-$25B targeted
items.diff_narrative: **Continuing: Cerebras IPO mid-May 2026 at $22B-$25B targeted — pre-IPO secondary at $26-$28B fully diluted, $510M 2025 revenue, $20B+/750MW OpenAI deal through 2028, underwriters Morgan Stanley/Citi/Barclays/UBS; first blockbuster AI IPO of 2026** — Carry-forward into the Apr 28 WSJ OpenAI-revenue-miss tape. Concentration risk on G42 (24%) + MBZUAI (62%) is the same overhang the Mag 7 prints just raised.

items.headline: DeepSeek V4 Pro / V4 Flash + Qwen 3.6 + Kimi K2.6 carry-forward
items.diff_narrative: **Continuing: DeepSeek V4 Pro / V4 Flash + Qwen 3.6 + Kimi K2.6 carry-forward — months-to-days Western/Chinese open-weight gap; first DeepSeek release optimized for Huawei Ascend chips** — Carry-forward; Bloomberg-style Western/Chinese open-weight catch-up frame holds into Mag 7 super-week.

items.headline: Hermes Agent v0.11.0 (Apr 23) + OpenClaw v2026.4.25-beta cadence + Anthropic Opus 4.7 (GA Apr 16) + Bonsai-8B + Unsloth 2026.4.8 (PyPI)
